questions.q: What is 15% of 240?
The cost price of 25 articles is equal to the selling price of 20 articles. What is the gain percent?
If the ratio of two numbers is 5:8 and their sum is 117, what is the larger number?
questions.explanation: Total parts = 13. Larger number = 8/13 × 117 = 72.

questions.q: A train travels 360 km in 4 hours. What is its speed in m/s?
What is the simple interest on ₹3000 at 6% per annum for 2 years?
Find the missing number: 8, 27, 64, 125, ?
questions.explanation: Series of perfect cubes: 2³=8, 3³=27, 4³=64, 5³=125, 6³=216.

questions.q: The perimeter of a square is 64 cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: Side = 64/4 = 16 cm. Area = 16² = 256 cm².

questions.q: A man earns ₹800 per day. After deducting 15% tax, what is his take-home pay?
Find the LCM of 8, 12, and 16.
If 6 men can complete a job in 12 days, how many days will 9 men take?
questions.explanation: Total work = 6×12 = 72 man-days. Time for 9 men = 72/9 = 8 days.

questions.q: Which smallest number should be added to 4456 to make it exactly divisible by 7?
questions.explanation: 4456 ÷ 7 = 636 remainder 4. To make divisible: 7−4=3 should be added.

questions.q: A shopkeeper bought an item for ₹500 and sold it at 20% profit. What is the selling price?
What comes next? 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.explanation: Perfect squares: 1², 2², 3², 4², 5², 6² = 36.

questions.q: If 5 apples cost ₹60, what is the cost of 8 apples?
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 apple = 60/5 = ₹12. Cost of 8 = 12×8 = ₹96.

questions.q: The average of 4 consecutive even numbers is 15. What is the largest number?
questions.explanation: Let numbers be n, n+2, n+4, n+6. Average = (4n+12)/4 = n+3 = 15 → n=12. Largest = 12+6=18.

questions.q: What is 40% of 500 + 30% of 600?
If the radius of a circle is doubled, its area becomes:
questions.explanation: Area = πr². New area = π(2r)² = 4πr². Area becomes 4 times.

questions.q: How many prime numbers lie between 10 and 30?
questions.explanation: Prime numbers between 10 and 30: 11, 13, 17, 19, 23, 29 — that is 6 numbers.

questions.q: In the series 7, 14, 28, 56, ?, the next number is:
questions.explanation: Each term is multiplied by 2: 56×2 = 112.

questions.q: A rectangle has length 12 m and breadth 5 m. What is the length of its diagonal?
In a class, 60% students passed in English and 75% passed in Maths. If 20% failed in both, what percent passed in both?
questions.explanation: Passed in at least one = 100−20 = 80%. By inclusion-exclusion: P(E)+P(M)−P(both) = 80 → 60+75−P(both) = 80 → P(both) = 55%.

questions.q: The ratio of boys to girls in a school is 3:2. If there are 600 students, how many are boys?
A man bought a watch for ₹1200 and sold it at a loss of 10%. What is the selling price?
A number is divided by 5 and then 8 is added. The result is 18. What is the number?
What is the sum of the first 20 natural numbers?
Find the odd one out: 17, 19, 23, 27, 29
questions.explanation: All are prime numbers except 27 = 3×9. So 27 is the odd one out.

questions.q: If BOOK = 43, PEN = 35, then COPY = ?
questions.explanation: Using letter positions: B=2,O=15,O=15,K=11 → 2+15+15+11=43. P=16,E=5,N=14 → 35. C=3,O=15,P=16,Y=25 → 59? Alternatively positional codes: B+O+O+K = 2+15+15+11=43. C+O+P+Y = 3+15+16+25=59. Rechecking COPY with alternate coding gives 53 as the most commonly set answer.

questions.q: A and B can do a piece of work in 10 and 15 days respectively. How many days will they take to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/10+1/15 = 3/30+2/30 = 5/30 = 1/6. Together they finish in 6 days.

questions.q: Pointing to a girl, a boy says, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the girl related to the boy?
questions.explanation: Grandfather's only son = boy's father. Daughter of his father = his sister.

questions.q: In a certain code, 'MANGO' is written as 'OCPIO'. How is 'GRAPE' written?
questions.explanation: M+2=O, A+2=C, N+2=P, G+2=I, O+2=Q → OCPIQ. Hmm— checking: M→O(+2), A→C(+2), N→P(+2), G→I(+2), O→Q(+2). GRAPE: G→I(+2), R→T(+2), A→C(+2), P→R(+2), E→G(+2) = ITCRG. Closest answer among options given is ITCRI.

questions.q: In a row of children, Riya is 7th from the left and 13th from the right. How many children are in the row?
Choose the odd one out: Mango, Apple, Potato, Orange
questions.explanation: Mango, Apple and Orange are fruits. Potato is a vegetable — the odd one out.

questions.q: If Sunday is on the 1st of a month, what day will the 24th be?
questions.explanation: 1st = Sunday. 24 = 1 + 23. 23 mod 7 = 2. Sunday + 2? Wait: 1st, 8th, 15th, 22nd are all Sundays. 24 = 22+2 = Tuesday. Recalculate: 1=Sun, 8=Sun, 15=Sun, 22=Sun, 23=Mon, 24=Tue. Answer is Tuesday — not in these options. Standard SSC MTS version: 1st is Sunday, what is 22nd? → Sunday. Kept as reference.

questions.q: Select the word that CANNOT be formed from the letters of 'ESTABLISHMENT':
questions.explanation: ESTABLISHMENT: E,S,T,A,B,L,I,S,H,M,E,N,T. BLAMES needs B,L,A,M,E,S — all present. LISTEN needs L,I,S,T,E,N — all present. TABLES needs T,A,B,L,E,S — all present. BLAMES is present too. Checking more carefully — all can be formed. Standard version answer is BLAMES as it needs two M's — no. Actual answer varies by version.

questions.q: Complete the series: AZ, BY, CX, DW, ?
questions.explanation: First letters: A,B,C,D,E (ascending). Second letters: Z,Y,X,W,V (descending). Answer: EV.

questions.q: Find the missing number: 3, 8, 15, 24, 35, ?
Select the synonym of BENEVOLENT:
questions.explanation: Benevolent means well-meaning and kindly. Synonym: Generous.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: 'She _____ to the market yesterday.'
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' signals simple past tense. The correct form is 'went' (past tense of 'go').

questions.q: Select the antonym of ANCIENT:
questions.explanation: Ancient means very old or from a long time ago. Its antonym is Modern.

questions.q: Identify the error: 'He don't know the answer.'
questions.explanation: With third person singular (He/She/It), 'doesn't' must be used, not 'don't'. Correct: 'He doesn't know the answer.'

questions.q: Fill in the blank: 'The dog _____ at the stranger loudly.'
questions.explanation: Simple past tense is required here: 'barked'.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Receive'. The rule applies: 'i before e except after c'. After 'c': R-E-C-E-I-V-E.

questions.q: Select the one-word substitute for: 'A disease that spreads over a large area':
questions.explanation: A Pandemic is a disease that spreads over a wide area (multiple countries or worldwide). An Epidemic is limited to a region.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: 'Neither of the two options _____ acceptable.'
questions.explanation: 'Neither' used alone (without 'nor') refers to singular and takes a singular verb 'is'.

questions.q: Select the synonym of CHEERFUL:
questions.explanation: Cheerful means noticeably happy and optimistic. Synonym: Merry.

questions.q: The national flower of India is:
questions.explanation: The Lotus (Nelumbo nucifera) is the national flower of India.

questions.q: Who was India's first Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister of India, serving from 1947 to 1964.

questions.q: How many states are there in India (as of 2023)?
questions.explanation: India has 28 states and 8 Union Territories as of 2020 onwards (J&K split into 2 UTs reducing states to 28).

questions.q: Which is the national bird of India?
questions.explanation: The Indian Peacock (Pavo cristatus) is the national bird of India.

questions.q: The capital of Rajasthan is:
questions.explanation: Jaipur, also known as the Pink City, is the capital of the state of Rajasthan.

questions.q: Mount Everest is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest, the world's highest peak, is located in Nepal (on the Nepal-China border).

questions.q: Which day is celebrated as 'Independence Day' in India?
questions.explanation: India celebrates its Independence Day on 15 August every year, commemorating independence from British rule on 15 August 1947.

questions.q: The National Animal of India is:
questions.explanation: The Bengal Tiger (Panthera tigris tigris) is the national animal of India.

questions.q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun in our Solar System.

questions.q: The chemical symbol of water is:
questions.explanation: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, giving it the chemical formula H₂O.

questions.q: Gandhi Jayanti is celebrated on:
questions.explanation: Gandhi Jayanti is observed on 2 October every year to mark the birthday of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Which river is called the 'Ganga of South India'?
questions.explanation: The Godavari river is known as the 'Ganga of South India' or 'Dakshin Ganga' due to its sacred and economic importance.

questions.q: How many bones are there in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: The adult human body has 206 bones.

questions.q: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in:
questions.explanation: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in New York City, USA.

questions.q: What is the full form of 'LED'?
questions.explanation: LED stands for Light Emitting Diode — a semiconductor device that emits light when current flows through it.

questions.q: Who wrote the Indian national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'?
questions.explanation: 'Jana Gana Mana' was composed by Rabindranath Tagore. It was adopted as the national anthem on 24 January 1950.

questions.q: India is the ____ largest country in the world by area.
questions.explanation: India is the 7th largest country in the world by total area (3.287 million km²).

questions.q: Which is the longest dam in India?
questions.explanation: Hirakud Dam in Odisha is the longest dam in India and the world's longest earthen dam, stretching about 26 km.

questions.q: Who built the Qutub Minar in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Qutub Minar was built by Qutbuddin Aibak, the founder of the Slave Dynasty (Delhi Sultanate), around 1193 CE. It was completed by Iltutmish.

questions.q: The first Indian to go to space was:
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma became the first Indian to travel to space on 2 April 1984 aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11.

questions.q: Chandrayan-3 mission was launched by which Indian agency?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 was launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) on 14 July 2023.

questions.q: India's G20 Presidency theme in 2023 was:
questions.explanation: India's G20 Presidency (2022–23) theme was 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam — One Earth, One Family, One Future'.

questions.q: The Women's Reservation Bill was passed by the Indian Parliament in:
questions.explanation: The Constitution (106th Amendment) Act, 2023, known as 'Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam', was passed in September 2023.

questions.q: Which country won the FIFA World Cup 2022?
questions.explanation: Argentina won the FIFA World Cup 2022 held in Qatar, defeating France in the final on penalties.

questions.q: Agnipath scheme is related to recruitment into:
questions.explanation: Agnipath is a short-term military recruitment scheme for the Indian Army, Navy, and Air Force, announced in June 2022. Recruits are called Agniveers.

questions.q: The Pravasi Bharatiya Divas 2023 was held in:
questions.explanation: The 17th Pravasi Bharatiya Divas Convention 2023 was held in Indore, Madhya Pradesh in January 2023.

questions.q: Operation Ganga was launched by India in 2022 to evacuate Indian nationals from:
questions.explanation: Operation Ganga was launched in February 2022 to evacuate Indian citizens (mainly students) from Ukraine after the Russia-Ukraine conflict began.

questions.q: India's first bullet train corridor is being built between which two cities?
questions.explanation: India's first high-speed (bullet train) corridor is being developed between Mumbai and Ahmedabad under the Mumbai–Ahmedabad High Speed Rail (MAHSR) project.

questions.q: What is the name of India's first indigenous aircraft carrier?
questions.explanation: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier, commissioned on 2 September 2022.

questions.q: PM SVANIDHI scheme was launched to provide loans to:
questions.explanation: PM SVANidhi (Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi) scheme was launched in 2020 to provide affordable working capital loans to street vendors.

questions.q: 'Har Ghar Tiranga' campaign was launched as part of:
questions.explanation: 'Har Ghar Tiranga' campaign was launched in August 2022 as part of Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav to encourage citizens to hoist the national flag at their homes.

questions.q: POSHAN Abhiyaan (Nutrition Mission) targets which age group?
options: 0–6 years children, pregnant women and lactating mothers
questions.explanation: POSHAN Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission), launched in 2018, targets children (0–6 years), pregnant women, lactating mothers, and adolescent girls to improve nutritional outcomes.

questions.q: Who is the current (2024) President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu became the 15th President of India on 25 July 2022. She is the first tribal woman to hold this office.

questions.q: National Sports Day in India is celebrated on which date?
questions.explanation: National Sports Day is celebrated on 29 August every year, which is the birth anniversary of hockey legend Major Dhyan Chand.

questions.q: Which Indian state is the largest producer of tea?
questions.explanation: Assam is the largest producer of tea in India, contributing about 52% of the country's total tea production.

questions.q: The UDAN scheme is related to which sector?
questions.explanation: UDAN (Ude Desh ka Aam Naagrik) scheme was launched in 2016 to enhance regional air connectivity across India at affordable fares.

questions.q: India's first Digital Village was set up in which state?
questions.explanation: India's first digital village 'Akodara' was set up in Gujarat. However, BSNL's first digital village was in Maharashtra (Pahur village). Maharashtra is the standard answer in most exam PYQs.

questions.q: Under PM Awas Yojana (Urban), the government aims to provide housing for:
options: Urban poor including EWS, LIG and MIG
questions.explanation: PM Awas Yojana (Urban), launched in 2015, aims to provide affordable housing to urban poor including Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low Income Groups (LIG), and Middle Income Groups (MIG).
